name: Mark Rober
oneLine: Former NASA JPL engineer who builds elaborate gadgets and large-scale science stunts on YouTube.
bio: Mark Rober spent nine years at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory, including seven on the Curiosity Mars rover, before going full-time on YouTube. His channel is built around heavily-produced, family-friendly engineering builds, viral stunts, and explainer-flavored science. He is best known for the annual porch-pirate Glitter Bomb series and the charity fundraising event #TeamSeas, and he co-founded the educational platform CrunchLabs.
style: highly-produced engineering build videos with multi-camera shoots, narration, and big reveal payoffs
